element_introduction_order.first_appears: after main game or tournament completion

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Round-based tension escalation with character-specific combo timing pressure; AI exploits create burst-intensity moments; tournament mode extends tension across 8-player gauntlet

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Stage selection cycles: UMK3 exclusive backgrounds appear twice before original MK3 stages cycle; password-selectable stages (Scorpion's Lair, Secret Cave, Abandoned River) accessible from Bank stage cycle
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden Portal stage from MK3 accessible via password; Noob's Dorfen stage requires Kombat Kode or prior completion; vertical stage transitions (Scorpion's Lair can uppercut into Kahn's Kave)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Standard arcade mode progression with base opponent AI; tutorial-level pacing with character selection freedom
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Improved AI with new exploitable behaviors (backflip jump-kick vulnerability, mimicry walking loop, corner-trap punishment); added combo damage rebalancing creates learning curve
difficulty_curve.late_game: Master difficulty unlocked after completing main game; 8-player Tournament mode extends challenge; Shao Kahn's Lost Treasures post-game content adds replay incentive
notable_spikes: Introduction of improved AI opponent behavior mid-progression
Exploitation of AI corner-trap flaws (creates false difficulty illusion)

notable_level_patterns.description: Scorpion's Lair/Hell features lava river Stage Fatality where uppercut sends opponent into environmental hazard; vertical level transition allows uppercut to knock opponents into Kahn's Kave above

7 new UMK3-exclusive backgrounds (Scorpion's Lair, Jade's Desert, River Kombat, Kahn's Kave, Blue Portal/Lost, Noob's Dorfen) cycle twice before original MK3 backgrounds appear in 1-2 player progression
Scorpion's Lair, Secret Cave, and Abandoned River accessible via Kombat Kodes from Bank stage cycle position; Balcony stage selectable via password without prior Noob Saibot defeat requirement
Progression cycles through UMK3 exclusive stages twice before entering original MK3 stage pool; creates predictable environmental variety while limiting player stage selection agency in arcade mode
